mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income and reduces consumption demand, which subsequently lowers equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS rise concurrently under the stated rate condition.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate hiring expansion, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates by more than 1.0 percentage point quarter-over-quarter, it signals a demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.0% qoq
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the deceleration signal, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and a federal funds rate above 1.5% indicates a policy-error regime where the central bank fails to cushion labor market weakness, amplifying recession fears and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak industrial output, a subsequent decline in housing starts signals a hardening of the business cycle, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when HOUST declines while INDPRO remains elevated above the threshold.
